title: Fatty Liver
tagline: Your liver is your chemical factory — don't overload it
oneLiner: Fatty liver means excess fat has built up inside liver cells. The liver can't clean your blood or process nutrients properly.

cards.title: What Does the Liver Do?
cards.body: Your liver is like a mega-factory — it filters toxins from blood, processes nutrients, makes bile for digestion, regulates blood sugar, and produces proteins. You cannot live without it.

cards.title: How Fatty Liver Develops
cards.body: When you eat excess calories (especially sugar and refined carbs), the liver converts the extra energy into fat. If this fat builds up faster than the liver can process it, fat deposits form inside liver cells.

cards.title: Alcoholic vs. Non-Alcoholic
cards.body: Alcoholic fatty liver is caused by alcohol damaging liver cells. Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) is increasingly common in India due to obesity, diabetes, and sugar-heavy diets — even without a drop of alcohol.

cards.title: Fructose is the Hidden Villain
cards.body: Fructose (found in sugary drinks, packaged juices, sweets) is processed almost exclusively by the liver. Large amounts overwhelm the liver and are stored directly as fat. Sugary drinks are a leading NAFLD cause.

cards.title: Grade 1, 2, 3 Explained
cards.body: Fatty liver is graded by severity on ultrasound. Grade 1 (mild) is reversible with lifestyle changes. Grade 3 (severe) can progress to cirrhosis (permanent scarring) if untreated.

cards.title: Weight Loss Reverses Fatty Liver
cards.body: Losing 7–10% of body weight significantly reduces liver fat — studies show Grade 1 NAFLD can completely resolve. No special medicine exists for NAFLD; diet and exercise are the treatment.

cards.title: Coffee Protects the Liver
cards.body: Surprisingly, 2–3 cups of plain black coffee daily are associated with lower rates of liver disease progression. Coffee contains antioxidants that reduce liver inflammation — but skip the sugar and milk.

doEat.name: Olive oil
doEat.note: Reduces liver enzymes and fat accumulation

doEat.name: Fatty fish (salmon, mackerel)
doEat.note: Omega-3 fatty acids directly reduce liver fat

doEat.name: Plain black coffee (2–3 cups)
doEat.note: Proven to slow liver disease progression

doEat.name: Lemon water
doEat.note: Vitamin C supports liver detox pathways

dontEat.name: Sugary drinks & packaged juices
dontEat.note: Fructose overloads liver — top NAFLD cause

dontEat.name: Maida & white bread
dontEat.note: Refined carbs converted to liver fat

dontEat.name: Fried foods
dontEat.note: Trans + saturated fat deposits in liver

dontEat.name: Red meat in excess
dontEat.note: Saturated fat adds to liver fat load

lifestyle: Lose 7–10% of body weight through diet and exercise — the single most effective treatment for NAFLD.
Completely avoid alcohol — even small amounts accelerate fatty liver progression.
Cut out sugary drinks including packaged fruit juice — replace with water, coconut water, or plain chaas.
Exercise 150–200 minutes per week (aerobic + strength training) — both types reduce liver fat.
Get a liver ultrasound and liver function tests (LFT) annually if you have grade 1 fatty liver.
Avoid unnecessary medications and supplements — many are processed by the liver and can worsen damage.
redFlags: Yellowing of skin or eyes (jaundice) — sign of liver failure
Severe pain in upper right abdomen
Swollen abdomen (ascites) — fluid accumulation from advanced liver disease
Extreme fatigue with nausea and no appetite for several days
Very dark urine (tea-coloured) or pale stool
Liver enzymes (SGPT/SGOT) more than 3x normal on blood test — seek urgent review

quiz.q: What is the main cause of non-alcoholic fatty liver (NAFLD) in India?
options: Excess sugar, refined carbs, and obesity
quiz.why: NAFLD is driven by excess calories — especially from sugar, refined carbs, and overall obesity — not alcohol.

quiz.q: Fatty liver (Grade 1) can be reversed with diet and exercise.
quiz.why: Grade 1 fatty liver is fully reversible. Losing 7–10% of body weight can eliminate liver fat deposits completely.

quiz.q: Which type of sugar is most directly linked to fatty liver?
quiz.why: Fructose is processed almost exclusively in the liver. Excess fructose from soft drinks and juices is directly stored as liver fat.

quiz.q: Plain black coffee (2–3 cups) is harmful to the liver.
quiz.why: Research consistently shows 2–3 cups of black coffee daily is protective for the liver, reducing inflammation and slowing disease.

quiz.q: The most effective treatment for NAFLD (non-alcoholic fatty liver) is:
options: 7–10% weight loss through diet and exercise
quiz.why: No approved medicine exists for NAFLD. Weight loss of 7–10% is the most evidence-backed treatment.
